title: Sterigmostemum incanum

This family consists of approximately 13,000 species, making it one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Its classification also places it within the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plants such as the gentians and the coffee family. Further classification of Sterigmostemum incanum places it within the subfamily Ixoroideae, which is characterized by its unique flower structure and arrangement.

intro: Sterigmostemum incanum, also known as the 'Brazilian guava', is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Sterigmostemum incanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
